title: Promising Penny Stocks To Watch In April 2026
content:
Over the last 7 days, the United States market has risen by 3.4%, and over the past year, it has climbed 30%, with earnings expected to grow by 16% annually. In such a thriving market, identifying stocks with strong financials becomes crucial for investors seeking value and growth potential. While penny stocks may seem like an outdated term, they still represent smaller or newer companies that can offer significant opportunities when backed by solid fundamentals.

Top 10 Penny Stocks In The United States

Name Share Price Market Cap Financial Health Rating ATRenew (RERE) $4.56 $1.04B ★★★★★★ WM Technology (MAPS) $0.4011 $69.45M ★★★★★★ LexinFintech Holdings (LX) $2.19 $375.23M ★★★★★★ FinVolution Group (FINV) $4.97 $1.31B ★★★★★☆ Tuniu (TOUR) $0.7079 $81.81M ★★★★★★ Information Services Group (III) $3.11 $194.03M ★★★★★★ Golden Growers Cooperative (GGRO.U) $5.00 $77.45M ★★★★★★ Niagen Bioscience (NAGE) $4.48 $369.14M ★★★★★★ LifeVantage (LFVN) $4.02 $52.12M ★★★★★★ SIGA Technologies (SIGA) $7.80 $328.13M ★★★★★★

Click here to see the full list of 361 stocks from our US Penny Stocks screener.

Let's take a closer look at a couple of our picks from the screened companies.

Hyliion Holdings

Simply Wall St Financial Health Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: Hyliion Holdings Corp. designs and develops power generators for stationary and mobile applications, with a market cap of $316.51 million.

Operations: Hyliion Holdings generates revenue from its Auto Parts & Accessories segment, totaling $3.48 million.

Market Cap: $316.51M

Hyliion Holdings, with a market cap of US$316.51 million, is navigating the penny stock landscape by focusing on its KARNO Power Module's commercialization. Despite reporting a net loss for 2025 and limited revenue of US$3.48 million from its Auto Parts & Accessories segment, the company remains debt-free and has short-term assets exceeding liabilities. Recent developments include successful diesel trials for the KARNO system and plans to raise up to $500 million through a shelf registration filing. While unprofitable with negative returns on equity, Hyliion aims for significant revenue growth in 2026 through R&D services and initial generator sales.

Click to explore a detailed breakdown of our findings in Hyliion Holdings' financial health report. Explore Hyliion Holdings' analyst forecasts in our growth report.HYLN Revenue & Expenses Breakdown as at Apr 2026

Ranpak Holdings

Simply Wall St Financial Health Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Ranpak Holdings Corp. offers product protection and end-of-line automation solutions for e-commerce and industrial supply chains across North America, Europe, and Asia, with a market cap of approximately $316.86 million.

Story Continues

Operations: The company generated $395 million in revenue from its product protection and automation solutions across North America, Europe, and Asia.

Market Cap: $316.86M

Ranpak Holdings, with a market cap of US$316.86 million, is navigating the penny stock landscape despite being unprofitable and experiencing increased net losses over the past year. The company reported revenue growth to US$395 million in 2025, driven by its product protection and automation solutions across key regions. While Ranpak's short-term assets cover its liabilities, its high net debt to equity ratio remains a concern. Recent strategic expansions include an enhanced partnership with Medline Industries to deploy advanced automation systems like Cut'it! EVO, aiming to boost operational efficiency and sustainability within healthcare distribution channels.

Unlock comprehensive insights into our analysis of Ranpak Holdings stock in this financial health report. Review our growth performance report to gain insights into Ranpak Holdings' future.PACK Debt to Equity History and Analysis as at Apr 2026

ATRenew

Simply Wall St Financial Health Rating: ★★★★★★

Overview: ATRenew Inc. operates a platform for pre-owned consumer electronics transactions and services in China, with a market cap of approximately $1.04 billion.

Operations: The company generates revenue from its Retail - Electronics segment, amounting to CN¥21.05 billion.

Market Cap: $1.04B

ATRenew Inc., with a market cap of approximately $1.04 billion, has shown significant progress in its financial performance, transitioning to profitability with a net income of CN¥336.29 million for 2025 from a previous net loss. The company’s debt management is robust, with short-term assets exceeding both short and long-term liabilities, and debt well-covered by operating cash flow. Recent strategic moves include share buybacks and dividend affirmations as part of its shareholder return plan. Despite low return on equity at 8.4%, ATRenew's earnings are anticipated to grow significantly, supported by seasoned management and board expertise.

title: G2 Investment Partners Initiates a New Position in Xometry
content:
According to a Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) filing dated Feb. 17,  G2 Investment Partners Management LLC reported a new stake in XometryXometry(NASDAQ:XMTR), acquiring 221,679 shares.

New position in Xometry Post-trade stake: 221,679 shares valued at $13.2 million Position represents 3.1% of the fund’s AUM

What else to know

Top holdings after the filing:

NASDAQ: DAVE: $29.4 million (7.0% of AUM) NYSE: PACK: $18.1 million (4.3% of AUM) NASDAQ: AEIS: $17.3 million (4.1% of AUM) NYSE: CLS: $14.7 million (3.5% of AUM) NASDAQ: VIAV: $14.7 million (3.5% of AUM)

Company Overview

Metric Value Revenue (TTM) $686.6 million Net Income (TTM) ($61.8 million) Price (as of market close Feb. 17) $55.83

Company Snapshot

Xometry is a leading digital marketplace for on-demand manufacturing, leveraging a broad partner network to deliver custom parts and assemblies at scale. Xometry enables a wide range of customers to source manufactured parts and assemblies using its technology-driven marketplace platform.

Offers a marketplace for sourcing manufactured parts and assemblies, including CNC machining, 3D printing, injection molding, and sheet metal fabrication services. Operates a digital platform that connects buyers with a network of manufacturing partners. Serves industries such as aerospace, automotive, electronics, medical, and industrial sectors, targeting product designers, engineers, and procurement professionals.

What this transaction means for investors

G2 Investment Partners quickly ramped up its position in Xometry to become one of its largest holdings. It didn’t hold any shares on Sept. 30, but its holdings, valued at $13.2 million, made up 3.1% of its assets under management (AUM) on Dec. 31, according to its SEC filing.

The asset management firm held 71 positions with an AUM of $420.7 million at year-end.

While the shares have dropped 34.8% this year (through March 17), they have still gained 50.9% over the last year.

Xometry is a small-niche company in the industry, but it has been growing its revenue rapidly. Fourth-quarter revenue increased 30% year over year to $192 million. The company grew the number of active suppliers and buyers by 17% and 20%, respectively.

Still, Xometry doesn’t report a profit under generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP). It lost $8.6 million in the fourth quarter versus a $9.9 million loss in the year-ago period.

At some point, the company will have to become profitable under GAAP to fuel long-term stock appreciation.

Should you buy stock in Xometry right now?
